tracking_rules.pp_main: Top 3 LESS per slate by |edge| magnitude + any MORE with proj − line ≥ 3.0 pts
tracking_rules.pp_spotlight: Biggest LESS per slate (highest-magnitude LESS, tracked separately)
tracking_rules.trap: NBA trap (from App.jsx NBADKTab): max(simOwn − val×K) where K = 25/medianVal, 25% simOwn gate; simulateFieldShowdown for ownership
tracking_rules.gem: NBA gem (from App.jsx NBADKTab): max(valueScore, replacerScore) where replacerScore requires same team + salary ≥ $3K; pivot is #2 overall if ≥25% of primary

featured_insight.headline: Trap fired 0/2 but pivot nailed 2/2 — fade-the-chalk misfired on both slates
featured_insight.detail: Model said fade the mega-chalk (Cade Cunningham 76.3% sim on ORL/DET, Wembanyama 83.5% sim on POR/SAS) — both ended up in winning lineups. 0/2 trap bust rate. But Hidden Gem's pivot slot hit BOTH times: Tobias Harris (ORL) and De'Aaron Fox (POR) were both in winning lineups while the gem primaries (Duren, Castle) missed. Pattern worth watching: on showdown slates the #2 gem candidate may be more reliable than #1. Biggest LESS spotlight split 1/2 — Wemby hit, Carter Jr. (biggest edge on either slate) missed and landed in the winner at 1.0% CPT draft.

pp_top_calls.note: Biggest edge on slate, biggest miss — 17/7/5 with 2 STL + 1 BLK in 37 min. In winning lineup at 1.0% CPT / 17.3% total draft — biggest leverage play of the night.
3 STL + 1 BLK (12 PP from stocks alone) + 3 made 3s pushed him past line
4 TOs in 22 min tanked his line — cleanest conviction hit

trap.result: MISS — 93.3% total draft chalk paid off in winner as UTIL (47.25 DK). Note: Wemby's CPT ownership was 53.3% — the field locked him in; bgreseth went UTIL which was the correct move.

pp_top_calls.note: Hit LESS but was in winning lineup at 93.3% total draft% — volume play paid off via 35 PTS + 5 3PM but only 5 REB / 1 AST kept total under high line. 2 BLK / 4 TO corrected from earlier box-score misread.
15 PTS / 4 3PM / 2 BLK — shot efficiently + stocks pushed him over
CPT of winning lineup (30 PTS, 10 REB DD) — our LESS call was dead wrong. bgreseth played him as 10.4% CPT leverage.

_notes: NBA v2 grading — full category set restored. Trap/gem use App.jsx NBADKTab's exact formulas (previous version dropped them incorrectly). Stats corrected: Wendell Carter Jr. (2 STL / 1 BLK / 0 TO, not 1/0/1 as I originally read — winning-lineup DK of 39.75 confirms) and Victor Wembanyama (2 BLK / 4 TO, not 4/2 — winning-lineup DK of 47.25 confirms). Both corrections preserved HIT/MISS outcomes but changed magnitudes. N=2, findings: (1) Biggest Trap missed on both slates — model's 'fade the mega-chalk' rule fires on showdown stars who often ARE the right play, not traps. Cade and Wemby both in winners. Structural concern. (2) Gem primary 0/2, gem pivot 2/2 — interesting pattern that warrants more data. Suggests #2 gem candidate may be the actionable pick. (3) PP top-3 LESS per slate: 2/6 hit = 33.3%. Biggest-LESS spotlight: 1/2 split. bgreseth won all 3 contests he entered (ORL/DET $7500 at 237.38 DK, POR/SAS $17400 at 242.50 DK, plus PHX/OKC $3028.58 — latter unarchived). On both archived slates he built around a differentiated CPT (Cade 32.8% CPT, Avdija 10.4% CPT = huge leverage on POR) paired with the top UTIL chalk + 3 value pieces. Worth studying as a meta-template for showdown.

slates_we_lack: 2026-04-19-phx-okc (image 7 — no slate JSON / PP / box score)
_study_candidate.pattern_observed: CPT at ≤33% ownership + top UTIL chalk + 2-3 sub-10% salary stackers. On POR/SAS the CPT (Avdija) was 10.4% and on ORL/DET (Cade) was 32.8%. Leverage at CPT slot is where most of the EV comes from.
